page: Amazon Web Services c7a.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro System. It features 4 dedicated physical cores utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU metrics placing in the top 10% and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%. Multi-core performance, such as stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, falls within the average tier. Memory performance is mixed; while cached small-block read bandwidth is average, uncached large-block read bandwidth and PassMark memory scores are positioned in the bottom 25%. Text processing and single-threaded bzip3 decompression show strong performance, while database operations and Redis SET throughput remain average.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c7a.xlarge offers high compute density with dedicated physical cores, making it highly efficient for CPU-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints. However, the low memory-to-vCPU ratio and weak uncached memory read bandwidth represent significant tradeoffs for memory-intensive workloads.

This instance is suited for compute-heavy workloads such as single-threaded processing, batch processing, web serving, software compilation, and lightweight machine learning inference tasks like small-model text generation. It is less suitable for large-scale databases or memory-intensive data analytics due to its memory constraints.
description: Amazon Web Services c7a.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro System, it includes 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The instance delivers strong single-core performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core benchmarks, and strong text processing and bzip3 decompression capabilities. However, its multi-core performance and uncached memory read bandwidth are average to weak. With a 2.0 GB per core memory allocation, the c7a.xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads but limited for memory-heavy applications. It is best suited for web serving, software compilation, batch processing, and lightweight CPU-based inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, delivering strong single-core performance for compute-bound tasks.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8.0 GB DDR5 RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and DDR5 memory for high-performance single-threaded and batch processing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 4 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 CPU cores running at 3.7 GHz
Equipped with 8.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory (2.0 GB per core)
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong text processing
Features a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps with no local storage
Optimized for web serving, software compilation, and lightweight CPU inference
